ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class; no specific shrimp-pork steamed dumpling subclass exists. Closest parent class used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers steamed dumplings broadly; no shrimp-pork specific code exists. Closest parent FoodEx2 anchor used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for steamed shrimp and pork dumplings identified in published SNOMED CT food hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of steamed shrimp and pork dumplings is 4 pieces, weighing approximately 80 g, as commonly served in a dim sum basket at Singaporean Chinese restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Steamed Shrimp and Pork Dumplings (虾肉蒸饺)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) — Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Food Composition Database lists 虾肉蒸饺 (Steamed Shrimp & Pork Dumplings) as a canonical entry with confidence 0.95. All per_100g values are taken directly from this source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for steamed shrimp and pork dumplings found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 48 (low) based on the closest analogous food: steamed wheat-flour dumplings with mixed protein filling, consistent with published GI values for wheat-based steamed dim sum items (GI range 45–55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (48) × carbohydrate per serving (8.03 g/100g × 80 g serving = 6.42 g) / 100 = 3.1 g. Estimation basis documented per Veritas source policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium at 46 mg per 100 g
hypertension.guidance: Steamed shrimp and pork dumplings contain only 46 mg sodium per 100 g, well below the 200 mg threshold; however, patients with hypertension should avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as soy sauce or chilli oil, and should request reduced seasoning when ordering at restaurants.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate (8 g/100 g) with negligible sugar (0.21 g/100 g) and high protein (8.71 g/100 g) moderating postprandial glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content (8 g/100 g), negligible sugar (0.21 g/100 g), and substantial protein (8.71 g/100 g) from shrimp and pork slow gastric emptying and moderate postprandial glucose response; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portions to 4–5 pieces per serving and avoid sweet dipping sauces to prevent glycaemic excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (3.78 g/100 g), low sodium (46 mg/100 g), and moderate cholesterol (28 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Steamed preparation minimises added fat; total fat is low at 3.78 g/100 g and sodium is very low at 46 mg/100 g, making this a heart-friendly dim sum choice, though individuals with cardiovascular disease should monitor portion size and avoid high-sodium or high-fat condiments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork filling contributes arachidonic acid (omega-6), which may amplify inflammatory cytokine production
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pork is a co-primary filling ingredient contributing arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid), which can amplify inflammatory pathways; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consider shrimp-only or chicken-filled alternatives and avoid high-fat dipping sauces.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Shrimp and Pork Dumplings (虾肉蒸饺, Xiā ròu zhēng jiǎo) are a classic Chinese dim sum item widely enjoyed in Singapore and across the Cantonese-speaking world. Each dumpling consists of a thin, translucent wheat-flour wrapper encasing a savoury filling of fresh shrimp (prawns) and minced pork, typically seasoned with ginger, sesame oil, and light soy sauce. The dumplings are cooked entirely by steaming, which preserves the natural sweetness and succulence of the shrimp while keeping the pork filling juicy and tender. The wrapper has a soft, slightly chewy texture, and the filling delivers a delicate balance of seafood sweetness and savoury pork umami. Per 100 g, these dumplings provide 101 kcal, 8.71 g protein, 3.78 g fat, and 8.03 g carbohydrate, with very low sodium (46 mg) and negligible sugar (0.21 g), making them one of the more nutritionally balanced dim sum options. The high protein content from both shrimp and pork supports satiety and moderates postprandial glucose response, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The low sodium content makes them suitable for individuals with hypertension when consumed without high-sodium dipping sauces. Steamed dumplings are a staple of yum cha (dim sum brunch) culture and are commonly served at lunch and dinner in Singaporean Chinese restaurants, hawker centres, and dim sum establishments.
common_user_queries: How many calories in steamed shrimp and pork dumplings?
Are steamed dumplings healthy for diabetics?
Is 虾肉蒸饺 suitable for hypertension?
What is the protein content of steamed shrimp dumplings?
Are steamed dumplings low in sodium?
Can I eat steamed dumplings if I have cardiovascular disease?
What is the GI of steamed dumplings?
How many pieces of dumplings is one serving?
Are shrimp and pork dumplings halal?
What allergens are in steamed shrimp and pork dumplings?

confidence_reason: All core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (Foodo), which is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. The input record carries a confidence of 0.95 with provenance marked as canonical and review_status as reviewed. The source match type is exact.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, dietary_fibre_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, fibre, vitamin, and moisture data for this specific steamed shrimp and pork dumpling preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry as of 2026. GI value of 48 is an analogous estimate based on steamed wheat-flour dumplings with mixed protein filling from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for this exact product was found. Glycemic load per serving calculated as GI (48) × available carbohydrate per serving (6.42 g) / 100 = 3.1. The dish is not halal-certified as it contains pork. FoodOn and FoodEx2 match types updated to 'closest_parent' to align with Veritas scoring model requirements for ontology honesty.
